name: box_exit
why: D-02: the staggered ghost box-exit (Inky immediately, Pinky ~+30, Clyde ~+60 frames) — freezes the exact frames ghosts cross the box boundary.

name: power_chase
why: D-02: power-pellet eaten, powerup active, ghosts flee (runaway targets) and slow to speed 1.

name: ghost_eat
why: D-02: a fleeing ghost is eaten during powerup (dead flag set, eat_freeze pause, score bonus).

name: death
why: D-02: Pac-Man caught by ghosts; runs to a natural game_over after all three lives are lost (D-19).

name: win
why: D-02: extended real dot-clearing gameplay. A true game_won requires clearing all 246 dots which is impractical to hand-script; this freezes a long real gameplay window (the safety cap is the soft-lock backstop, D-19).

name: box_edge
why: D-02 + Phase 3 BUG-01: ghosts pressed against the box boundary — captures the initial exit AND the post-death reset that puts ghosts back inside the box and re-exits across the box edge. These are the exact frames Phase 3's box-edge fix changes.

name: tunnel_wrap
why: D-02 + Phase 2 wrap math: Pac-Man crosses the left/right tunnel wrap edge (x -> -47/897). Phase 2 touches the wrap arithmetic; this freezes the exact wrap frame.

name: eyes_return
why: D-02: an eaten ghost (dead flag True) travels the move_clyde dead-ghost return path back toward the box. Phase 2 collapses this fallback; this freezes the eyes-return trajectory.

name: claude_session_01
why: D-10/HRN-04: one recorded Claude-played session (a pure greedy eat-dots / grab-powerups / hunt-ghosts policy) serialized to sparse input so it replays deterministically in CI.
